creator: antonio@triforce.info
text:
Tobias,

I see the e-mail you sent me. I actually submitted this port submission back in January. Is there a reason it took ten months to hear back on a status of this submission as that seems awfully long to hear back on a port submission. When I did the testing of this port back in January every command ran properly with no errors. I actually do not even have the test FreeBSD operating system installed on my computer any more as I am running a production OpenBSD desktop on my computer now. Back in January I had FreeBSD 10.3-RELEASE AMD64 installed on my computer and I did section 3.4 Testing the port successfully from the Freebsd.org Handbook. Again no problems encountered.

This is the post I made on the freebsd.org fourms back in January as I sought assistance from fellow FreeBSD users from the freebsd.org forums as I had not done a port submission before.

https://forums.freebsd.org/threads/59046/page-2

"Great news gentlemen I was able to do section 3.4 Testing the port successfully on the FreeBSD porting handbook. I am now on the final section 3.6 submitting the new port. I will be submitting the port soon. Thanks for all your help. This is my first time submitting a port to FreeBSD."

My question to you why did the testing complete fine in January but now you in October are telling me that there are problems? Also since it's been ten months since I heard anything in regard to this submission what is it that you want me to do as I do not even have this software installed on my computer any more and I now have production software on that test hard drive I used in January.

Regards,

Antonio Gandara
